'''Saber 3''' was an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]]<ref name="CCG" /> which was manufactured by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]].<ref name="DB" /> Part of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing's]] [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]]<ref name="CCG" /> by [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="date1">The ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] is set around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Saber 3 was [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]]<ref name="CCG" /> by [[DS-181-3]], who flew as the [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] of Saber Squadron's [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]], watching Fel's back.<ref name="CCG1">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=DS-181-3}}</ref> In Saber 3's capacity of flying as Fel's wing, the [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] was often ordered to remain at a distance from the [[Baron/Legends|baron]] in order to give<ref name="CCG" /> him<ref name="CCG1" /> maneuvering room during [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]].<ref name="CCG" />

In 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="date2">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> DS-181-3 fought in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|212|DS-181-3}}</ref> where [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] starfighters attempted to destroy the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] while [[TIE pilot]]s attempted to defend the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] from the Alliance starfighters. The battle ended in an Imperial defeat and the Death Star's destruction.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> Saber 3 bore a bloodstripe<ref name="CCG" /> which was the standard Saber Squadron insignia, representing a minimum of 10 [[Death/Legends|kills]].<ref name="CCG2">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 1}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Saber 3 was created for a card in [[2000]]'s ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Decipher]] [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]],<ref name="CCG" /> and was simultaneously mentioned on DS-181-3's card for the same set.<ref name="CCG1" />
